cases.input: BTC has refused to break lower, and I think it will rally again over the next 30 days. US equities have fallen sharply, and capital may be rotating back into crypto.

cue_scaffolds.summary: Persistent demand may make relative resilience appear before the broader trend expands
If risk assets resume synchronized deleveraging, short-term resilience may be only a temporary dislocation

cases.example_interview: You may be seeing more than a bullish BTC setup: as US equities release pressure, BTC's refusal to fall is itself becoming a capital-allocation choice. One aligned Cue emphasizes persistent buying absorbing supply, while a contrasting Cue warns that synchronized deleveraging could erase the resilience. Do you want to develop the early rotation or the still-firm demand? Neither is fine; if there is nothing more, I will proceed with your version.
cases.example_completion_check: Two angles could sharpen this: if capital is rotating, BTC's relative strength versus Nasdaq should keep widening; if this is only a short-term dislocation, the next synchronized risk-off move should quickly reverse it. Add either one, or keep your current version.

cases.input: Nvidia's earnings were strong, and I think it will keep rising over the next five days. Make me a Frame.

cue_scaffolds.summary: Continued upward earnings revisions could extend the post-earnings rerating
The next step may be expectations following through across suppliers and peers, not the company result itself

cases.example_interview: I hear more than simply good earnings: you think the post-earnings rerating is unfinished. One aligned Cue follows continued upward revisions, while an adjacent Cue waits for suppliers and peers to follow. Which transmission matters more to you, or are you seeing another force? If there is nothing more, I will proceed with this.
cases.example_completion_check: One unspoken point could help: for the five-day view to hold, the market may need to carry the result across the AI chain rather than merely digest the same report again. Add it or keep the current version.

cases.input: I have long been bullish on gold and think it will keep rising over the next three months. Help me express the view.
cases.example_interview: You did not just turn bullish; you think this long-held view has reached a moment worth amplifying. What recently changed that makes now different from earlier or later? If there is nothing more, I will proceed with this.

cases.input: I am bullish on oil over the next 30 days. Hormuz is unstable, and insurance and rerouting costs may move before any supply interruption.
cases.example_interview: You are seeing the risk premium move through the cost chain before supply is actually interrupted. If this begins to play out, will you first watch freight rates, the futures curve, or relative strength across energy assets? If there is nothing more, I will proceed with this.

cases.input: The market is focused on AI capex, but I think MU's real catalyst over the next 30 days is gross-margin recovery.
cases.example_interview: Your edge is not simply watching AI; it is believing the market is focused on the wrong transmission outcome. Is the market most likely to underestimate the size of the margin recovery, its duration, or its valuation impact? If there is nothing more, I will proceed with this.

cases.input: I am bullish on BTC over the next 30 days. It stayed relatively strong while US equities fell, spot demand held firm, and marginal capital may be rotating early. I want to watch BTC's relative strength versus Nasdaq.
cases.example_interview: This view is already complete: it has an anomaly, a mechanism, and a next signal. In the final expression, what must not be diluted: BTC's resilience, the cross-asset capital rotation, or the explicit 30-day call? If there is nothing more, I will proceed with this.

forbidden_moves: Turn news, signals, and intuition into a single questionnaire
Ask the user to prove the view or begin by stating what would prove it wrong
Write an assistant-suggested mechanism as the user's own view before the user adopts it
Use Cue count, popularity, ranking, or directional agreement as factual proof or social pressure
Write another creator's Cue in the current user's first-person voice without adoption
Ask for a target price during an ordinary fast preview
Restart the Cue interview or completion check after the user skips it
